severity_name: SEVERE SLOP WARNING — SYNTHETIC VIDEO SURGE ADVISORY
headline: SEVERE SLOP WARNING: TEXT-TO-VIDEO FRONT ERUPTS ACROSS THE YOUTUBE SHORTS GORGE AS FABRICATED FOOTAGE OF WEEPING CATS, TRAMPOLINING LIVESTOCK, AND EVENTS THAT NEVER HAPPENED ACHIEVES FULL-MOTION SATURATION
advisory: A fast-developing synthetic-video system has moved out of the still-image lowlands and into full motion, generating photorealistic clips of things that were never filmed because they never occurred. Residents are advised that footage now moves, blinks, and cries on cue, and that the traditional 'it looks too real to be fake' heuristic has been formally deprecated. The Service reminds the public that a video's emotional impact carries no information about whether it is true. Verify before you share; the pause button is a public-safety device.

regional_outlook.conditions: Zero visibility as a wall of nine-second serialized cat melodramas rolls through on autoplay. Grotesque animal drama advisories remain in effect; parents are urged to shelter children on the far bank.
Warm, humid, and thick with reposted 'real security camera footage' that no camera recorded. Comment sections report widespread earnest concern for animals that do not exist.
Gusty. Fabricated clips are circulating faster than corrections can be issued, with the label 'context added' arriving days downwind.
A dozen near-identical 'make any video' tools crest the charts, each promising cinema and delivering a talking hamster in a courtroom.
Calm and clearing. Newly deployed content-limiting filters are reducing accumulation to manageable levels; residents report actual visible bottom.

specimen.status: Explosively common; monetized until removed, then re-uploaded under a new name by nightfall.
specimen.habitat: Vertical-video gorges and the outer Facebook Delta, breeding wherever autoplay meets a soft heart.
specimen.field_marks: Photorealistic cat with unnaturally symmetrical tears, a human-cadence voiceover narrating its tragic backstory, and a cliffhanger caption reading 'Part 14 — you won't believe what happens next.' Eyes do not track light correctly; fur occasionally passes through furniture.
specimen.call: They said I was too small to survive the storm — but Mama never gave up on me. Follow for Part 15.
specimen.confusion_species: Easily mistaken for a genuine rescue-animal video, which also features cats and sincerity. Tell them apart by continuity: a real cat has the same number of whiskers between shots, does not narrate its own thoughts in complete sentences, and is not serialized like a soap opera.

spotter_tip: If a video makes you feel a great deal while telling you almost nothing verifiable — no place, no date, no name — you are likely observing slop in full plumage. Scrub the timeline back and forth; synthetic footage tends to flicker at the seams when you refuse to just keep scrolling.
tomorrow: Continued heavy synthetic video with a chance of talking-dog courtroom dramas; brief clearing where platform filters hold.

confirmed_sighting.report: An industry analysis reports that nearly 10 of one platform's 100 fastest-growing channels in 2025 were AI-generated, and that the rise of grotesque 'cat soap opera' content aimed at children has prompted YouTube to demonetize or remove some channels.
